response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior engineer with strong software craftsmanship, testing culture, and some high-throughput systems exposure, but he falls well short of the core requirements for this role. The position demands 8 years of C++ and deep large-scale cloud/compute infrastructure leadership; his C++ experience is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments and a thesis project, with his systems work mostly in DLang and his recent career centered on TypeScript/Node.js product development. He also lacks formal technical-leadership experience managing cross-team priorities on a platform of this scale.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput microservices experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus mentoring via workshops and courses
Broad full-stack and DevOps breadth, including Kubernetes, Docker, and open-source platform ownership (GISCollective)
concerns: Nowhere near the required 8 years of C++ experience; systems work is primarily in DLang
No formal technical-lead role leading strategy, priorities, and cross-team alignment at scale
Recent experience (last ~5 years) is web/product engineering, not cloud compute platform or infrastructure architecture

key_factors.factor: Limited cloud platform / technical leadership track record
